per_skill.semantic_dup_note: Almost none of this skill's reference content is semantically duplicated by SKILL.md: SKILL.md is a deliberately thin (~80-line) decision skeleton whose own gate (check-skill-core-headroom) pushes all vocabulary into references, and the catalog.yaml/planner is the real router. The required/scope primers (quality-lenses, gate-classification, automation-promotion, proposal-flow, maintainer-local-enforcement, index) are INLINE-class: SKILL.md names the concepts (lenses, enforcement tier, gate classification, recommended moves) but never defines the load-bearing enums/tokens/fields a run must emit, so they are not re-statable from SKILL.md. The remaining 32 are genuinely trigger-gated DEPTH. Lexically the small overlap that exists (e.g. the local-proof invariant, the cautilus/prompt-asset pointers) is invisible to clone tools because SKILL.md uses one-line paraphrases, not the ref bodies. No DUP and no DEAD references found.

references.name: contract-modes.md
references.evidence: SKILL.md 'Contract Shaping' inlines all three modes: 'Choose the lightest honest contract shape', 'When implementation churn would be expensive, reduce ambiguity earlier' (upfront), 'If some answers will emerge only while building, keep the contract probe-friendly' (braided), 'If the repo already treats executable checks as contract artifacts, push acceptance into those checks' (executable). The ref itself states these are 'shaping heuristics... not meant to become a mandatory user-facing mode menu', i.e. elaboration not load-bearing artifact.

synthesis.systemic_pattern: The impl pattern (references that semantically duplicate SKILL.md, invisible to lexical clone tools) is PERVASIVE, but its true size is understated if you count only the DUP bucket. Across 24 skills / 196 references, the redundancy a run already holds without re-reading is DUP + INLINE = 26 + 58 = 84 references = 42.9% of all references. DUP (13.3%) is restatable from SKILL.md prose; INLINE (29.6%) is where SKILL.md names/cites the concept but a load-bearing token still lives only in the ref. Add the 3 DEAD refs and 44.4% (87/196) are NOT trigger-gated depth a run must open on demand; only DEPTH (109, 55.6%) is genuinely on-demand. The impl-specific framing ('5/8 are semantic duplicates') generalizes: 12 of 24 skills have >=50% of references in the redundant DUP+INLINE+DEAD class (achieve, markdown-preview, gather-notion, web-fetch, gather-slack all 100%; issue 83%; create-skill and spec 75%; handoff 71%; ideation 67%; hotl 67%; announcement and hitl 60%). So the disease impl flagged is the modal case for small-to-mid skills, not an outlier. Magnitude caveat: pure DUP (fully restatable) is only 13.3% — the dominant redundancy form is INLINE, where SKILL.md has effectively absorbed a ref but left 1-3 exact tokens stranded. The fix is therefore rarely 'delete the ref' and usually 'lift the stranded tokens into SKILL.md, then delete or demote.'
synthesis.dup_fraction_finding: Pure DUP is 26/196 = 13.3% of all references — content whose entirety is semantically restatable from SKILL.md, and lexically invisible (every dup_note reports clone tools see ~0% overlap because the ref rewords the same intent). That 13.3% is the delete-on-sight, no-information-loss floor: those refs add reading cost and a coverage obligation while carrying nothing a run needs. But DUP alone undercounts redundancy: INLINE (29.6%) is content SKILL.md already cites/absorbed where only a small exact token (enum, template field, flag) was left in the ref. Treat DUP as 'remove' and INLINE as 'lift-then-remove/demote' and the combined 'run already has this' share is 42.9% (84/196). Meaning: roughly two of every five charness references do not earn their place as on-demand depth — they either repeat SKILL.md (DUP) or strand a token SKILL.md should own (INLINE). The clone-tool blind spot is total: none of these 84 are detectable by lexical duplication tooling, so any 'references are not duplicated' signal from a diff/clone scanner is a false negative across the entire corpus.

synthesis.floor_metric_finding: YES — the declaredReferences-coverage floor systematically mismeasures, confirmed against scripts/agent-runtime/build-skill-execution-observation.mjs (lines 658-683, 737). Coverage = covered.length/declared.length where covered = declared refs the run OPENED (Read tool file_path/notebook_path, or a file operand of cat/sed/head/tail/less/nl/rg/grep/awk). The run summary cautilus scores then emits 'Reference coverage: X/Y declared references opened (missing: ...)' and names every unopened ref as a deficiency. This punishes a run for not RE-OPENING the 84 DUP+INLINE references whose content it already has inline in SKILL.md — exactly the impl complaint. The bias is worst for the high-redundant-share skills (achieve, markdown-preview, gather-notion, web-fetch, gather-slack all 100% redundant; issue 83%; create-skill/spec 75%; handoff 71%), where a perfect lean run that never wastefully re-reads scores far below 100% and looks worse than a wasteful run that cat's every ref. It is also DEAD-blind: the 3 DEAD refs are declared, so coverage demands opening lineage trivia no run should read. Two structural facts soften but do not excuse it: (1) coverage is surfaced as ADVISORY, not the pass/fail outcome — outcome is driven by requiredCommandFragments/requiredSummaryFragments (the floor matcher, lines 663-666; the line 39-45 comment confirms coverage is 'the secondary metric'); (2) the 4 floor_risk skills (retro, release, impl, issue) are where coverage-as-floor pressure most distorts behavior. Net: as a SCORED signal in the A/B harness (run_skill_efficiency_ab.py consumes this canonical per-run file), coverage rewards wasteful re-reads and penalizes lean runs — it measures re-read obedience, not correctness, and should not gate or rank runs.

recommendations: DELETE the 26 pure-DUP refs outright (no information loss; clone tools can't see the overlap so nothing depends on it). Priority: gather-notion (both), web-fetch (runtime-contract+routing-table), gather-slack (runtime-contract), spec (8 paraphrased step-refs), ideation (4 of 8 paraphrased refs).
DELETE the 3 DEAD refs (web-fetch provenance, gather-slack provenance.md, release lineage) — they are declared, so they currently inflate the coverage denominator for content no run should read.

For the 58 INLINE refs, LIFT-THEN-DEMOTE: move the 1-3 stranded tokens into SKILL.md, then delete the ref or strip it to residual depth and remove it from declaredReferences. Start with validator-enforced tokens (issue closeout-discipline/resolution-brief, impl verification-ladder, quality automation-promotion/gate-classification) since stranding those risks real closeout-validation failures.

REPLACE the declaredReferences-coverage floor: it measures re-read obedience, not correctness. Either (a) drop coverage as a scored/ranked signal and keep only requiredCommandFragments/requiredSummaryFragments (the real outcome floor), or (b) restrict the coverage denominator to DEPTH-classed references only (the 109 on-demand refs) so a lean run that never re-reads DUP/INLINE scores 100%. Tag each reference with its class (DUP/INLINE/DEPTH) in catalog/frontmatter so the matcher excludes non-DEPTH automatically.

EXPECTED END STATE after compression: ~109 DEPTH refs survive as references/; DUP/DEAD deleted (-29); INLINE tokens lifted into ~17 SKILL.md bodies (-58 refs). Re-run check-skill-core-headroom afterward — quality's own gate pushes vocabulary OUT of SKILL.md, so lifting tokens may collide with its headroom budget; keep only load-bearing enum/template tokens inline and leave prose elaboration deleted.

FIXTURE PHILOSOPHY: stop treating 'all declared references opened' as a fidelity fixture. A correct run for a low-DEPTH skill (achieve, markdown-preview, the gather integrations) opens ZERO references and that is right. Fixtures should assert the run EMITTED the right tokens (now inline) and opened a DEPTH ref only when its trigger fired — never assert raw open-count. Special: achieve has zero DEPTH refs, so its entire references/ tree should collapse into SKILL.md + inline-lifts of adapter-contract/goal-artifact/lifecycle, possibly leaving an empty references/ dir.
